message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step plan:
1) Parse main nodes and identify any explicit cross-references.
2) Compare the target policy chunk against each main node’s requirements (with their cross-references where applicable).
3) Detect deviations per taxonomy. Treat omissions as Non-Deviation per rule 4.
4) For any deviation, quote exact spans from the provided regulation text to substantiate.

Assessments:

A) REG-092 (Art77(1))
- Core requirement: Right to lodge a complaint “with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement” if the data subject “considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.”
- Policy text: “In accordance with Art 77 GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes data protection law.”
- Differences:
  • The regulation ties the right to an infringement of “this Regulation.” Quote: “infringes this Regulation.”
  • The policy broadens to “data protection law,” expanding beyond GDPR to any data protection law.
- Per taxonomy, this is an over-compliance/expansion relative to the minimum scope required by GDPR. Classify as Severity deviation (policy is more permissive).
- Omitted details (Member State of habitual residence/place of work/alleged infringement; “without prejudice...” preface) are omissions, not deviations.

B) REG-093 (Art77(2))
- Core requirement: Supervisory authority must inform complainant on progress and outcome, including the possibility of a judicial remedy pursuant to Article 78.
- Cross-reference: Article 78 (we have 78(1), 78(3), 78(4) in referenced_nodes and 78(2) as a main node).
- Policy chunk is silent on this supervisory authority obligation. As per rule 4, omissions are Non-Deviation. No contradictory or narrowed phrasing is present.

C) REG-095 (Art78(2))
- Core requirement: Right to effective judicial remedy where the competent supervisory authority does not handle a complaint or fails to inform within three months on the progress or outcome; this refers to a complaint “lodged pursuant to Article 77,” and competence per Articles 55 and 56.
- Cross-references available: Article 77 (REG-092, REG-093). Articles 55 and 56 are not provided; do not assume them.
- Policy chunk is silent on judicial remedy and the three-month trigger. Per rule 4, omissions are Non-Deviation.

D) REG-021 (Art15(1))
- Core requirement: Right of access and a detailed list (a)-(h), including (f) “the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority,” and cross-references to Article 22(1) and (4) for automated decision-making; we have 22(1) available (REG-041).
- Policy chunk only mentions the complaint right generally (as Art 77), not within an access-right context and not the rest of (a)-(h). This is an omission under the provided taxonomy and thus Non-Deviation (no contradictory statements are made).
- No narrowing or negation of any item (a)-(h) is present.

Quotations from regulations for the deviation (REG-092):
- From Art77(1): “...if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.”
- Policy states “infringes data protection law,” which is broader than “this Regulation” (GDPR), creating an over-permissive scope compared to the minimum requirement.

Outputs below follow the required JSON schema, listing considered cross-references.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "analysis": "Article 77(1) states the complaint right applies where processing \"infringes this Regulation.\" Quote: \"...if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.\" The policy states complaints may be lodged if processing \"infringes data protection law,\" broadening the trigger beyond GDPR itself. This is over-compliant relative to the minimum scope specified by Art77(1)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-093",
    "clause": "Art77(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-094", "REG-095", "REG-096", "REG-097"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-095",
    "clause": "Art78(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-092", "REG-093"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
